title: Respond at personal-ledger scale
description: Interactive External Orchestrator operations remain responsive for the personal dataset without a daemon, remote cache, or external database.
target: At 100000 active transactions plus retained evidence and history, first-page filtered actuals with full pool/category aggregates and reconciliation candidate or completed-scope projections complete within 2 seconds at p95; ordinary single-entity writes complete within 1 second at p95, excluding backup, restore, and migration.
load_condition: Three accounts, 100000 active transactions, two Spend Pool, representative category, payment-attribution, evidence, reconciliation, correction, and relationship history, one OS identity, one writer, and no concurrent load on the supported reference workstation.
measurement: Thirty warmed repetitions per representative actuals, reconciliation-candidate, coverage, and ordinary write scenario using monotonic elapsed time; report p50 and p95.
rationale: This gives design a measurable local baseline for the expanded Ledger while preserving the accepted complexity budget.
